data.title: A member of the WLFI Wallet team claims that USD1 will become one of the top three stablecoins within two months.
data.description: A user named Dylan_0x (@0xDylan_), suspected to be a member of the WLFI Wallet team, posted on the X platform, stating, "Within two months, USD1 will be among the top three stablecoins; this is not speculation." It is understood that WLFI announced yesterday that it will soon release a series of announcements regarding its ecosystem and the continued growth of USD1. CoinGecko data shows that USDT and USDC currently rank second and third in stablecoin market capitalization with $186.78 billion and $74.77 billion respectively, USDS ranks third with a market capitalization of approximately $9.78 billion, while USD1 currently has a market capitalization of approximately $3.43 billion, ranking seventh among stablecoins.

data.title: Regulatory Improvements Key to Institutional Adoption of Crypto Assets
data.description:
According to Odaily, Goldman Sachs analysts, including James Yaro, have highlighted the importance of regulatory improvements in driving institutional adoption of crypto assets. This development is particularly beneficial for both buy-side and sell-side financial institutions and is expected to foster new applications for crypto assets beyond trading. The analysts emphasized that the U.S. Clarity Act, currently progressing through Congress, serves as a crucial catalyst.

The report suggests that the Clarity Act will establish a clear regulatory framework for tokenized assets and decentralized finance (DeFi), delineating the responsibilities of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). This clarity is essential for unlocking institutional capital and encouraging compliant participation. Goldman Sachs cautions that the act needs to be passed by the first half of 2026 to avoid potential delays due to the U.S. midterm elections in November.

Previously, Tim Scott, the Republican chairman of the Senate Banking Committee, indicated that the committee would soon revise the Clarity Act and move it to the voting stage. Industry experts also noted that while market adjustments towards the end of 2025 might slow short-term adoption, successful implementation of the act could significantly accelerate genuine institutional entry.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: US Crypto Market Structure Act Could Be a Key Catalyst for Institutional Entry
data.description: Goldman Sachs analysts, including James Yaro, stated in a report that the continued improvement in the regulatory environment is becoming a key factor driving further institutional adoption of crypto assets, particularly benefiting both buy-side and sell-side financial institutions. This will also promote the development of new application scenarios for crypto assets beyond trading. Analysts point out that the Clarity Act, currently being pushed forward in Congress to reform the US crypto market, is a significant catalyst. The report argues that this act will clarify the regulatory framework for tokenized assets and decentralized finance (DeFi), and clearly define the regulatory responsibilities of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), a necessary prerequisite for releasing institutional capital and promoting compliant participation. Goldman Sachs also cautions that the bill needs to be passed in the first half of 2026; otherwise, the US midterm elections in November could delay the legislative process. Previously, Republican Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott stated that the committee would soon revise the Clarity Act and enter the voting stage. Industry insiders also point out that although market adjustments at the end of 2025 may slow short-term adoption, if the bill is successfully passed, it could significantly accelerate genuine institutional entry. (Forbes)

data.title: The US Solana spot ETF saw no net inflows and remained stable.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (January 9th, Eastern Time), the Solana spot ETF saw no net inflows. All eight Solana spot ETFs remained unchanged throughout the day, with neither net inflow nor outflow. As of press time, the Solana spot ETF's total net asset value was $1.09 billion, with a net asset value ratio of 1.43%. Historically, it has seen a cumulative net inflow of $817 million.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a net inflow of $4.93 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $4.93 million yesterday (January 9th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday (January 9th, Eastern Time) was the Franklin XRP ETF (XRPZ), with a single-day net inflow of $4.53 million and a historical total net inflow of $277 million. This was followed by the Grayscale XRP ETF (GXRP), with a single-day net inflow of $404,000 and a historical total net inflow of $263 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs was $1.47 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.16%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $1.22 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin OG long positions have paper losses exceeding $9 million, with $5.56 million in funding fees already paid.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, as the market continues to decline, the long positions held by the well-known Bitcoin OG (10/11) have now incurred floating losses exceeding $9 million, while also accumulating losses of approximately $5.56 million in funding fees. Specifically, the BTC and ETH long positions held by this address are currently both in a loss-making state, while the SOL long position remains profitable.

data.title: FBI Warns of North Korean Cyber Group's Malicious QR Code Attacks
data.description: According to Foresight News, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) has issued a warning about the North Korean cyber group Kimsuky, which is reportedly using malicious QR codes in targeted phishing attacks. The group's targets include think tanks, academic institutions, and organizations involved in diplomacy and international affairs. The attacks involve impersonating well-known figures in the field of foreign policy and embedding QR codes with malicious links in emails. These codes are designed to lure victims into switching from their computers to mobile browsers, thereby bypassing email security measures.
